shorts.title: ​‘The Office US’ cast: Where the Dunder Mifflin folks are now​
shorts.title_distilled: ​‘The Office US’ cast: Where the Dunder Mifflin folks are now​.
shorts.description: Over ten years since the last episode, ‘The Office US’ is still one of the all-time favorites. This mockumentary about the oddball staff at Dunder Mifflin in Scranton, Pennsylvania, first hit screens in 2005 and stuck around for nine seasons, wrapping up in 2…
shorts.content: Over ten years since the last episode, ‘The Office US’ is still one of the all-time favorites. This mockumentary about the oddball staff at Dunder Mifflin in Scranton, Pennsylvania, first hit screens in 2005 and stuck around for nine seasons, wrapping up in 2…
shorts.content_distilled: Over ten years since the last episode, ‘The Office US’ is still one of the all-time favorites.

shorts.title: Scranton organization celebrates giving back
shorts.description: SCRANTON, LACKAWANNA COUNTY (WBRE/WYOU) — A local organization is ready to donate a big check they raised. The Frank Burke the Fourth Memorial Fund is celebrating another year of giving back. They raised $40,000 to be donated to Make-A-Wish, and $8,000 more for South Scranton Intermediate School. The Bogey for Burkey's golf tournament was held [...]

shorts.description: SCRANTON, LACKAWANNA COUNTY (WBRE/WYOU) — After mass, runners and walkers laced up their sneakers for the Brian P Kelly Memorial Race in Scranton. The two-mile foot race takes place entirely on the parade route and is the oldest road race in the Scranton area. The race is held in honor of Brian P Kelly, the [...]

shorts.title: Annual tradition held before Scranton Saint Patrick's Parade
shorts.title_distilled: Annual tradition held before Scranton Saint Patrick's Parade.
shorts.description: SCRANTON, LACKAWANNA COUNTY (WBRE/WYOU) — Before the parade stepped off, another annual tradition was held in downtown Scranton. Saint Peter's Cathedral hosted its annual Saint Patrick's Parade Day Mass on Saturday morning. Bishop Bambera led the mass, and various priests from the diocese of Scranton concelebrated. "As a Christian, it's very important. It keeps everyone [...]
